author: @phredkat7799
text: Great timing!  I just got several old oxygen cylinders that I want to cut down for large wind gongs. This simple jig will make the cuts easy and accurate. Thanks Robert!

author: @__WJK__
text: Just to reiterate NEVER cut, grind, drill or weld on any containers that contain flammable materials! That said, love the channel, keep the great content coming!

author: @huthutchinson1778
text: G'day Robert. Please examine how to reduce wind noise on microphones when video taping outside.  I am sure you will be able to come up with something that will not be too expensive. :)   Merry Christmas.

author: @stevecummins324
text:
I've wondered if one could use similar as driven end of half decent large throw lathe . Run say an old car  wheel, or large cylinder on casters. Belt drive around wheel and pulley on motor to both keep drum pulled tight on casters. And provide torque increase.
 Use 4 tapped holes with bolts perpendicular to axis of rotation as a simple "4 jaw independent chuck"

author: @salimufari
text: Love the arrangement with one exception. Getting your body out of the path of a shattering disk & arm out of the path of a kickback's recoil. Simply moving your position to the end of the bench so no part of you is in the plane of the disk wouldn't be that hard at all from this example. As a builder & fabricator when one of those abrasive disks goes flying it's very unsettling to say the least.

author: @kelvinsparks4651
text: That's a super simple and effective idea,  well done  . I don't want to be one of those health and safety geeks but from my own personal experience please use ear defender's.  I have tinnitus through years of negligence and its not nice having a high pitch ringing every waking hour.  Stay safe mate an wishing you , luke and your families a happy Christmas and New year 😊

author: @noonehere1793
text: Brilliantly efficient and simple….well done sir👍👍

author: @jb-dn3ct
text: If you don't ask you don't get, that set up looks really effective, although the thing I like most is that you have different angle grinders with different wheels on, I'm not going to lie for me changing discs becomes a bit of a chore 😂

author: @donniewatson9120
text: I saw a setup like this to cut 55-gallon drums. He had a hacksaw bound in the arm and a motor that turned the drum through the wheels that the drum could roll on. He had a slight bit of weight that kept pressure between the hacksaw blade and the drum. He had an adjustable fence to keep the drum alignment with the distance he wanted to cut off the drum at. He would set it up and let it go until the blade made its way through the drum wall. It worked real well.

author: @ibme8359
text: Your's is a bit bigger than the one I built to cut plastic bottles! Thanks for the tips to size it up a BIT!!!

author: @weekendstuff

author: @overbuiltautomotive1299

author: @mikaelhg
text: How to build reusable test benches and cleaning stands etc. out of simple materials might be an interesting topic. Thanks for this video, since a lot of people spend a lot of time cursing annoying tasks, when they simply haven't been reminded that building such a jig or holder for just their task is something that they themselves can do.

author: @davidt5776
text: The fumes off the grinding discs and aluminium are toxic, so you should really have a mask and have a ventilated room. I also reminder to make the gas cylinders safe before cutting into them. Otherwise you will have a bang of a time.

author: @jimbobarooney2861
text: Any simply  ideas on how to cut 45 degrees, its something I need, its plastic pipe that wont fit into a bandsaw

replies.author: @stevecummins324
replies.text: Not perfect, but it's usually possible to use formulas involving trig in   excel/open office to create graphs that can be scaled then printed onto paper to wrap around pipe, to use as a cutting template.

replies.author: @jimbobarooney2861
replies.text: @stevecummins324 that's the way we've being using, surface development on acad, print scale 1:1 and use as a template
